title: Stream
description: A named, partitioned, append-only sequence of records hosted by a streaming platform (e.g. a Kafka topic, Pulsar topic, Kinesis stream, Pub/Sub topic). Describes how the stream is shaped on the wire, not its payload schema.

id.description: Stable, platform-scoped identifier for the stream (URN, ARN, FQTN, etc.).

name.description: Human-readable name of the stream / topic.

description.description: Free-text description of what the stream contains.

platform.description: The streaming platform hosting this stream.

namespace.description: Multi-tenant namespace, project, or tenant under which the stream lives (Pulsar tenant/namespace, GCP project, etc.).

partitions.description: Number of partitions / shards. Bounds parallelism for consumers.

replication_factor.description: Number of replicas maintained per partition for durability.

retention.description: Retention policy for records in the stream.

duration.description: ISO 8601 duration if mode is 'time' (e.g. P7D).

max_bytes.description: Maximum total bytes if mode is 'size'.

ordering_key.description: The record key (or partition-key strategy) used to assign records to partitions and to define ordering scope.

delivery_guarantee.description: Delivery guarantee offered by the platform configuration for this stream.

schema.description: Reference to the record schema for this stream.

registry.description: URL of the Schema Registry holding the schema.

tiered_storage.description: Whether older segments are offloaded to object storage.
